scope: Marina and harbour basics batch merged into starter.json after Content Producer draft, Sea Speak Linguist review, Director-Engineer matcher fix, and QA acceptance.

qa_notes.linguist_note: Confirmed strict: keep berth separate from dock, quay, pier, and slip across Batch 002.
Draft keeps fuel berth as the formal target and rejects fuel dock/pier.
Word item trains the service noun. Phrase items handle fresh-water wording.
Electricity is intentionally rejected as ordinary paraphrase.
Confirmed strict: fender is the trained Sea Speak term. Finder and bumper must stay wrong.
Reject rope for this Sea Speak drill; line is the trained mooring term.
Alongside means next to berth/vessel, not merely abeam.
Keep movement verbs separate for early harbour drills.
Depart is a verb; this word item trains the noun departure.
Mooring, berth, and anchor are kept separate in this routine harbour draft.
Confirmed safe item-local variant: request a berth preserves formal berth-request meaning. Informal need a berth stays wrong.
Fuel berth remains distinct from generic fuel request or ordinary dock wording.
Confirmed safe item-local water-service variant: request fresh water may answer request water. Fuel and shore power stay wrong.
Draft rejects power/electricity as loose paraphrases.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: stand by outside / do not answer
qa_notes.linguist_note: Keep stand by outside separate from wait out and do not answer.
Confirmed safe item-local variant: optional the preserves meaning. Do not accept go ahead as a movement instruction here.
Alongside alone is a word item; this expression requires the action come alongside.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: port side to / starboard side to
qa_notes.linguist_note: Do not accept left side to; Sea Speak side term must be trained.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: starboard side to / port side to
qa_notes.linguist_note: Do not accept right side to; Sea Speak side term must be trained.
Confirmed strict: prepare fenders is an instruction; fenders ready is a status report and must stay wrong for this item.
Confirmed strict: prepare lines is an instruction; lines ready is a status report and must stay wrong for this item.
Confirmed safe item-local departure-request variant: request permission to depart preserves routine marina departure meaning.
Keep approach/proceed/enter distinct unless linguist approves item-local variants.
Lines secured reports a different state after mooring; keep wrong for this preparatory expression.

items.source_text: Marina Control, это парусная яхта Orion.
items.target_text: Marina Control, this is sailing yacht Orion.
accepted_answers: marina control this is sailing yacht orion
marina control this is sy orion
marina control this is s y orion

should_accept: Marina Control, this is sailing yacht Orion.
Marina Control this is S/Y Orion
should_reject: Marina Control this is motor yacht Orion
qa_notes.linguist_note: Follows starter called-station-first policy. S/Y variants follow Batch 001 precedent.

items.source_text: Запрашиваю место у причала на сегодня вечером.

qa_notes.linguist_note: Confirmed safe item-local variants: requesting and optional article follow starter berth-request style.

should_reject: do not answer outside the marina
dangerous_minimal_pairs: stand by outside / do not answer
qa_notes.linguist_note: Optional the is safe; wait out and do not answer must remain wrong.

items.target_text: I am arriving from the south.
accepted_answers: i am arriving from the south

should_accept: I am arriving from the south.
should_reject: i am departing to the south
i am arriving from the north
qa_notes.linguist_note: Missing article before south is accepted as a small grammar slip; direction must stay exact.
Visitor berth is distinct from resident berth; reject dock wording.

items.source_text: Запрашиваю место у причала на одну ночь.
accepted_answers: request a berth for one night
requesting a berth for one night

should_accept: request a berth for one night
should_reject: need a berth for one night
qa_notes.linguist_note: Duration must remain one night. Mooring is not accepted as berth here.

accepted_answers: request a fuel berth on arrival
requesting a fuel berth on arrival

should_accept: request a fuel berth on arrival
qa_notes.linguist_note: Keeps fuel berth distinct from generic fuel and normal berth.
Confirmed safe item-local water-service variant: request water may answer request fresh water in this routine marina context.
Draft rejects power/electricity as too loose for this service term.

accepted_answers: request fresh water and shore power

should_accept: request fresh water and shore power
qa_notes.linguist_note: Requires both services. Adding fresh before water is safe here because both water and shore power remain present.

items.source_text: Следуйте к месту Bravo one two.
items.target_text: Proceed to berth Bravo one two.
accepted_answers: proceed to berth bravo one two
proceed to the berth bravo one two

should_accept: Proceed to berth Bravo one two.
proceed to the berth Bravo one two
should_reject: proceed to berth bravo two one
go ahead to berth bravo one two
proceed to dock bravo one two
dangerous_minimal_pairs: Bravo one two / Bravo two one
qa_notes.linguist_note: Confirmed strict for Batch 002: keep spoken-form Bravo one two only. Compact B12/B-12 needs Director-Engineer matcher QA before acceptance.
Confirmed safe item-local variant: approach slowly preserves the slow-speed approach instruction. Reduce speed remains a different instruction.

items.target_text: Stand by outside the harbour entrance.
accepted_answers: stand by outside the harbour entrance

should_accept: Stand by outside the harbour entrance.
should_reject: wait out outside the harbour entrance
stand by inside the harbour entrance
qa_notes.linguist_note: Location outside/inside must remain exact.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: port side to / starboard side to
qa_notes.linguist_note: Accepting short port side to is risky but practical; left side to stays wrong.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: starboard side to / port side to
qa_notes.linguist_note: Accepting short starboard side to is risky but practical; right side to stays wrong.
Bumpers remains rejected. Side must not be fuzzed.

accepted_answers: prepare bow line and stern line

should_accept: prepare bow line and stern line
qa_notes.linguist_note: Confirmed strict: this item is an instruction to prepare lines. Bow and stern lines ready is a status report and stays wrong.
Alongside and speed adverb must remain exact.
Confirmed safe item-local variants: request departure and request departure permission preserve the routine departure-permission request.
Ready to depart is a readiness report, not an actual departure report.

items.source_text: Я отхожу от места у причала.

should_reject: i am arriving at the berth
qa_notes.linguist_note: Dock remains rejected to preserve berth terminology.

items.source_text: Ожидайте снаружи входа в марину для назначения места.
items.target_text: Stand by outside the marina entrance for berth assignment.
accepted_answers: stand by outside the marina entrance for berth assignment
stand by outside marina entrance for berth assignment

should_accept: Stand by outside the marina entrance for berth assignment.
stand by outside marina entrance for berth assignment
should_reject: wait out outside the marina entrance for berth assignment
enter the marina entrance for berth assignment
stand by inside the marina entrance for berth assignment
qa_notes.linguist_note: Advanced only because of phrase density. No port-control authority language intended.

items.source_text: Следуйте к месту три, левым бортом к причалу.
items.target_text: Proceed to berth three, port side to.
accepted_answers: proceed to berth three port side to
proceed to the berth three port side to

should_accept: Proceed to berth three, port side to.
proceed to the berth three port side to
should_reject: proceed to berth three starboard side to
go ahead to berth three port side to
proceed to dock three port side to
dangerous_minimal_pairs: port side to / starboard side to
qa_notes.linguist_note: Combines berth number and side-to instruction; side and proceed verb must remain strict.

items.source_text: После получения топлива следуйте к вашему месту у причала.
items.target_text: After taking fuel, proceed to your berth.
accepted_answers: after taking fuel proceed to your berth
after fuel proceed to your berth

should_accept: After taking fuel, proceed to your berth.
after fuel proceed to your berth
should_reject: after taking water proceed to your berth
after taking fuel go ahead to your berth
after taking fuel proceed to your dock
qa_notes.linguist_note: After fuel is accepted as a shortened form; service type and berth terminology remain strict.

items.target_text: Depart when the fairway is clear.
accepted_answers: depart when the fairway is clear

should_accept: Depart when the fairway is clear.
should_reject: depart when the berth is clear
enter when the fairway is clear
qa_notes.linguist_note: Confirmed appropriate for routine marina scope: a conditional fairway-clear departure instruction, not distress or VTS authority language.

items.source_text: Подходите медленно и держите кранцы готовыми.
items.target_text: Approach slowly and keep fenders ready.
accepted_answers: approach slowly and keep fenders ready

should_accept: Approach slowly and keep fenders ready.
should_reject: proceed slowly and keep fenders ready
approach slowly and keep lines ready
approach quickly and keep fenders ready
qa_notes.linguist_note: Combined phrase ties approach and mooring prep. Reject proceed unless linguist approves as item-local variant.

grammar_patterns.pattern: Marina station + this is + vessel name
examples: Marina Control, this is sailing yacht Orion.
Stand by outside the harbour entrance.
I am arriving from the south.

grammar_patterns.pattern: Request + service one + and + service two

examples: Proceed to berth Bravo one two.

grammar_patterns.pattern: Stand by outside + location + for berth assignment
examples: Stand by outside the marina entrance for berth assignment.

grammar_patterns.pattern: Proceed to berth + berth number + side to
examples: Proceed to berth three, port side to.
After taking fuel, proceed to your berth.
Depart when the fairway is clear.

grammar_patterns.pattern: Approach + manner + and keep + mooring item + ready
examples: Approach slowly and keep fenders ready.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Berth is the harbour place; birth is an ordinary English word and must not pass through typo matching.
Confirmed strict for Batch 002: berth stays separate from dock, quay, pier, and slip.
Mooring, berthing, and anchoring are related but not interchangeable in this batch.
Line is the trained mooring term; rope remains wrong for these drills.
Finder is a one-letter ordinary word and bumper is marina slang; both must stay wrong for the trained Sea Speak fender term.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: port side to / starboard side to
dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Port and starboard side-to instructions are operationally opposite and must not be fuzzed or translated to left/right.
Relative positions and alongside mooring language must stay distinct.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: stand by outside / wait out / do not answer

phrase_marina_stand_by_outside_001: do not answer outside the marina

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Stand by outside is a harbour waiting instruction, not wait out or do not answer.

Harbour movement verbs have different operational meanings; go ahead is a radio permission phrase, not a movement synonym here. Compact B12 is also rejected until alphanumeric berth formats receive matcher QA.
The draft trains formal request wording and rejects informal need phrasing.
Routine marina services must not substitute for each other.
Arrival and departure are opposite workflow states.

producer_notes.note: Linguist decisions: request a berth/requesting berth are safe item-local variants; request fresh water and request water are mutually safe only for water-service items; fenders ready and lines ready are status reports and were removed from prepare-command items; request departure permission/request departure are safe item-local departure-request variants; approach slowly and after fuel remain safe item-local shortenings.

Confirmed rejected across Batch 002 unless a future Director-Engineer task says otherwise: dock, quay, pier, slip, rope, bumper, electricity, power, need a berth, left side to, right side to, and compact B12 berth identifiers.

Protect one-letter or near-token changes: berth/birth, fender/finder, line/rope, port/starboard, outside/inside, tonight/tomorrow, one night/two nights, Bravo one two/Bravo two one, and spoken Bravo one two/compact B12.
No policy change requested by Content Producer. If Linguist wants broad harbour synonym acceptance for dock/quay/pier/berth, route to Director-Engineer before integration.
